editableNewDialogue.text: If I put a small fireproof pouch inside a safe deposit box, is there any downside?

Usually it’s fine, but there are a few potential downsides: the pouch can trap moisture (bad for paper or electronics), it can take up extra space in a small box, and some “fireproof” pouches off-gas odors or residues over time. If you use one, choose something rated for documents/media, let it air out first, and consider adding a small desiccant packet (silica gel) to control humidity.
Would silica gel ever be a problem for the bank?

Typically no—small sealed desiccant packs are common in storage. The main “problem” is practical: if a packet leaks beads or dust it’s messy, so pick intact packets and avoid the indicator types that can shed. Also keep everything sealed so nothing spills when the box is opened.
Got it. Also, why do so many box sizes sound like they’re measured in riddles?

Because they kind of are: banks often advertise nominal sizes (e.g., “3x5”) that don’t reflect usable interior space once you account for the drawer lip, hinges, and rounding. Asking for the interior dimensions—or even a paper template—usually clears up the mystery.
Paper template sounds smart. Random fact time: I heard the New York Public Library has millions of items.

It does—NYPL’s research collections are enormous (tens of millions of items across books, manuscripts, maps, photos, and more). Fun aside: some libraries also store archival film in cold rooms because low temperatures slow chemical decay.
Since I'm swinging by the branch anyway, I’ll probably just grab one of those little 5x10 boxes so I don’t have to play Tetris with the case again.
That sounds like a practical choice—going a bit larger can save you hassle fitting the case and leave room for padding.
By the way, those “bank you / on the money / interest” lines were just me being corny in the moment—I don’t actually like punny replies back.
Got it. I’ll avoid leaning into puns and keep my tone straightforward unless you specifically ask for wordplay.
Yeah—please keep it direct. No more pun follow-ups from your side.
Understood: direct responses, and I won’t add puns.
